_comment: The estate map: the one artifact that genuinely cannot live in any single repo or a generic plugin. It is the assembled view of every repo, how each deploys, and how they depend on one another. The assistant reads this to (1) discover which repo holds a capability, (2) assemble a per-project working set, and (3) know the safety meaning of an action in each repo. Each repo's row mirrors what that repo declares in its own repo-manifest.json — this file is the aggregation.

environments._comment: Cross-account credential map — spans more than one cloud account family, so no single repo owns it.

branch_model_semantics._comment: Why a push means different things in different repos — the safety taxonomy a coordinating session needs loaded for ALL repos at once.
branch_model_semantics.trunk: A push to the main branch deploys to STAGING only; production is gated by a separate manual approval. Pushing is safe any day.
branch_model_semantics.dev-then-trunk: main IS production. A direct push to main is a production deploy. The sanctioned path is promotion from dev after review + staging validation.
